Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for DOT audit and hazmat compliance threads using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance" or similar). Finds: Linda Castellano's formal PHMSA inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting hazmat documentation for MoveOps shipments, Marcus Thorne's forward to Chloe flagging four hazmat shipments in scope, Chloe's reply listing the four in-scope moves (Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 lithium, Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B laser, Raj Patel / NorthWind / lithium UPS, Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack / oversize rig, not hazmat), Marcus's extension request to DOT, and Castellano's extension approval granting a revised deadline of June 5, 2026.
OE 2: Search emails for Swift Relocations and Greg Pallone correspondence using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "placarding" or similar). Finds: Chloe's urgent request to Greg for the signed Tanaka placarding certificate, Greg's reply confirming placarding was completed at transport by driver Mike Becerra on April 15 (job #SR-2026-04-1538) with scanned PDFs to follow within 24 hours, and Greg's separate pricing email announcing a 15% base rate increase and $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment for post-audit compliance work. The pricing email is the source for the "Swift documentation surcharges" Marcus has been asking about.
OE 3: Search Slack #operations channel for messages from Javier and Suki using conversations_search_messages (query: "placarding" or "elevator" or "reassign" or "Tanaka" or "Reyes" or similar, filter_in_channel: C006). Finds three injected messages: (a) Javier's April 25 message reporting that the Swift placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka was sent via overnight courier on April 22 but USPS tracking shows it was returned to sender due to an incorrect zip code on the MoveOps office address, Greg Pallone is resending but cannot guarantee delivery before end of week. (b) Suki's April 24 message saying she is currently assigned to 8 active relocations with three having hard deadlines, Deshawn Hartley was supposed to deliver by April 19 and they missed it, Simone Richter has an urgent lease situation, and Terrence Nakamura has a 9200-lb split shipment needing coordinated pickup from two vendors on the same day, requesting reassignment of lower-complexity moves. (c) Javier's April 25 message about the Joaquín Reyes move to Seattle, building management informed them the freight elevator at the destination office is reserved for another tenant move from April 14 through April 18, overlapping the delivery window of April 16–18, and the 900-lb rig cannot go up stairs, delivery must be pushed to April 19 or later or negotiated with building management.
OE 4: Search Linear for the DOT compliance project and its issues using linear_list_projects (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or similar) and then linear_list_issues (project: "project_dot_compliance_audit"). Finds project "DOT Compliance Audit, Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments" (state: started, lead: Chloe Vance) with five issues: OPS-1 (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, "DOT Docs, Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium", state_done, all four doc categories verified), OPS-2 (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic, "DOT Docs- Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser", state_in_progress, priority 1, missing signed placarding certificate, description attributes gap to Blessing Okafor who had only verbal confirmation from Swift driver), OPS-3 (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state_done), OPS-4 (issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack, "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig", state_done, not classified as hazmat under 49 CFR 171-180, flagged for overweight/oversize permit only, carrier Heartland Movers), and OPS-5 (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations - URGENT", state_todo, priority 1).
OE 5: Read comments on DOT tickets OPS-2 and OPS-3 using linear_list_comments. On OPS-2 (Tanaka): finds Chloe's April 22 comment (comment_chloe_swift_confirmation) noting Greg confirmed placarding, driver Mike Becerra, job SR-2026-04-1538, scanned PDFs expected within 24 hours / by EOD April 23. On OPS-3 (Patel): finds Chloe's comment (linear_comment_093d903c5cb9) disputing closure, she cannot find a signed custody log in the move packet, vendor email trail, or Victor Huang's April 10 escalation materials. The Airtable record still shows Status = In Progress while Linear shows Done. She flags this as a documentation integrity problem, closure without actual proof of custody. This means OPS-3 was closed prematurely and needs to be corrected.
OE 6: List all relocation records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to assess coordinator workload. Finds active (In Progress) moves by coordinator: Javier Morales has 8 active- Rachel Dominguez (Canopy, Apr 7-9), Sasha Greenwald (PivotPoint, Apr 18-20), Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop, Apr 21-23), Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy, Apr 12-14), Priya Suresh (Vectral, Apr 14-18, ESA German Shepherd), Joaquín Reyes (GreenStack, Apr 11-19, 900-lb solar rig), Diego Castañeda (PivotPoint, Apr 12–May 2, 600-lb CNC mill), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, Apr 15-23, hazmat laser cutter). Suki Patel has 9 active- Daniel Reeves (Vectral, Apr 10-12), Maya Johansson (Vectral, Apr 14-16), Andre Mitchell (Mosaic, Apr 22-24), Simone Richter (BrightLoop, Apr 4-6, urgent lease), Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, Apr 14-18, Honda Civic vehicle shipping), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral, Apr 16-22, ADA, 9200-lb split shipment), Camille Oduya (Vectral, Apr 12-14, tight 2-day window), Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic, Apr 14-19, Automate 2026 trade show deadline), Jae-won Kim (StormCloud, Apr 7-9). Fatimah Al-Rashidi has ~5 active - Tomás Herrera, Lena Kowalski, Dr. Renata Kovacs (TN visa/Canada customs), Mei-Lin Zhao (Class 9 lithium hazmat), Nadia Okonjo (H-1B visa). Blessing Okafor has ~5 active - Kevin Okafor, Preethi Nair, Marcus Webb (Canopy, cryogenic lab equipment), Raj Patel (NorthWind, Class 9 hazmat chain-of-custody), Emilia Cruz. Agent identifies which moves are non-transferable (hazmat, hard client deadlines this week, Blessing's DOT-documentation files) and which have flexible timelines for reassignment. Likely reassignment candidates: Jordan Whitfield (no special handling, from Javier to Fatimah or Blessing) and Andre Mitchell (no hazmat, from Suki to Fatimah).
OE 7: Look up email addresses for Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, and all four coordinators using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Elena Rostova", "Marcus Thorne", "Javier Morales", "Suki Patel", "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", "Blessing Okafor"). Finds elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, javier.morales@moveops.com, suki.patel@moveops.com, fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, blessing.okafor@moveops.com.
OE 8: Look up the #operations channel ID using channels_list. Finds channel C006, name "operations."
OE 9: Check Chloe's calendar for availability this week (April 26–May 2) using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start: 2026-04-26, end: 2026-05-02) to find an open 30-minute slot for the coordinator sync.
OE 10: Update stale or incorrect DOT tickets using linear_update_issue. OPS-3 (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind): change state from state_done back to state_in_progress because the signed custody log has not been located per Chloe's own comment disputing the closure. OPS-5 (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert): update description or add comment noting the cert was returned to sender per Javier's Slack message and delivery is uncertain before end of week. Agent may also add comments using linear_create_comment to OPS-2 or OPS-5 noting the returned-to-sender development.
OE 11: Create a new Linear ticket for the Joaquín Reyes delivery/elevator conflict using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations, title: "Joaquín Reyes Seattle Delivery - Freight Elevator Conflict" or similar, description: freight elevator reserved by another tenant April 14–18 overlapping delivery window, 900-lb rig cannot use stairs, need to push delivery or negotiate building access, assignee: Javier Morales, priority: urgent or high).
OE 12: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to reassign moves from Javier and Suki to coordinators with more bandwidth. Reassignment candidates must respect constraints from the prompt: no moves with active hazmat, no hard client deadlines this week, and no DOT-documentation-scoped moves reassigned to Blessing. Likely reassignments: Jordan Whitfield (recReloc00000035, from Javier to Blessing or Fatimah) and Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000038, from Suki to Fatimah).
OE 13: Send email to Elena Rostova about DOT audit status using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipient: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Content includes per-shipment documentation status for all four PHMSA-flagged shipments: (1) Mei-Lin Zhao - complete, all docs verified, (2) Keiko Tanaka - placarding certificate confirmed by Swift but signed original returned to sender due to incorrect zip code, being resent, not yet in hand, (3) Raj Patel - ticket was marked complete but signed custody log cannot be located, under investigation, (4) Joaquín Reyes - complete, not hazmat, overweight/oversize docs on file. Includes assessment of whether June 5 deadline is achievable. Does NOT mention coordinator staffing issues or reassignment plans per the prompt constraint.
OE 14: Post the reassignment plan in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006). Content includes which moves were reassigned, from which coordinator to which, the new load distribution, the rationale (workload rebalancing), and that no hazmat or hard-deadline moves were touched.
OE 15: Create a 30-minute calendar event for the coordinator sync using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Coordinator Sync - Reassignments & DOT Audit Timeline" or similar, duration: 30 minutes, scheduled for a slot this week April 26–May 2). Attendees: Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com), Fatimah Al-Rashidi (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com), Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com), Javier Morales (javier.morales@moveops.com), Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com), and Marcus Thorne (marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Marcus is included as per the prompt: "Marcus should probably be on that too since he has been handling the DOT response on the finance side."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

This satisfies the 'not received' or 'still outstanding' criteria. However, the agent did not discover the returned-to-sender/incorrect zip code detail from Javier's message, so it reports the cert as merely delayed rather than returned to sender.

✓Run #6The email body explicitly states the physical copy 'was RETURNED TO SENDER due to an incorrect zip code on the MoveOps office address' and that the scanned PDF 'NOT RECEIVED as of April 26.' The Tanaka placarding cert is clearly identified as not in hand.

20 The model must use airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to retrieve relocation records for coordinator workload assessment.

23 When querying Airtable, the model must target the Relocations table (tblRelocations01, or a table name containing 'Relocation' or similar) to retrieve coordinator assignments and move details.
